mnemonics: My Crazy Great Engineers Really Understand Trees And Codes Now
King Raccoons Love Delicious Incredible Hamburgers Quickly Often Eating Nachos
keyPoints: Prim's Algorithm: Start with an arbitrary node, add the minimum edge weight to the tree, repeat until all nodes are connected.
Kruskal's Algorithm: Sort edges by weight, add the smallest edge to the tree if it doesn't form a cycle.
Dijkstra's Algorithm: Start from a source node, select the minimum distance node, update distances and edges, repeat until the source is reached.
Huffman Trees: Binary trees with weighted edges, used for compression and coding, priority encoding followed by prefix coding.
Greedy Method: Make the locally optimal choice at each step, hoping it leads to a global optimum solution.
